user.title: Noonlight credentials
user.description: Enter your Noonlight API token and choose which environment to target. Use sandbox for testing — it never alerts real responders.

data_description.api_token: Long-lived token from your Noonlight account portal.
data_description.environment: production reaches real responders. sandbox is safe for testing. Choose custom to point at your own endpoint.
data_description.base_url: Only used when environment is 'custom'; paste the full base URL for your endpoint.

caller.title: Caller and location
caller.description: Sent to Noonlight with every dispatch so responders know who and where. Phone, state, and ZIP are validated now to avoid a confusing failure later, at dispatch time.

data_description.name: Name responders should ask for, e.g. Jane Doe.
data_description.phone: Callback number. Any common US format works — (202) 555-0142, 202-555-0142, or +12025550142 — and is stored as +12025550142.
data_description.address: Street address only, e.g. 123 Main St.
data_description.state: Two-letter US state/territory code, e.g. VA (not 'Virginia' or 'va').
data_description.zip: 5-digit US ZIP, e.g. 62704 (ZIP+4 like 62704-1234 also accepted).
data_description.location_id: Label for this property if you run multiple, e.g. 'Site A' or 'Lake House'. Sent to Noonlight with every alarm so you can tell which site raised it. Leave blank if you only have one.

defaults.title: Defaults
defaults.description: Tune the grace window, de-dup window, and which dispatch services to enable.

data_description.default_entry_delay_seconds: Grace window before a dispatch fires. Cancelable during this time.
data_description.dedupe_seconds: After a dispatch fires, repeat dispatches within this window are ignored.
data_description.services_granted: Only the selected dispatch services will be available.

data.safety_ack: I understand this integration can summon real police, fire, and medical responders, and that false dispatches may incur fines from local authorities.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Noonlight
reauth_confirm.description: Your Noonlight token was rejected. Paste a new token to restore service.

reconfigure.title: Edit caller and location
reconfigure.description: Update the caller details, address, or site label sent to Noonlight. Phone, state, and ZIP are re-validated.

data_description.name: Name responders should ask for, e.g. Jane Doe.
data_description.phone: Callback number. Any common US format works — (202) 555-0142 or +12025550142.
data_description.address: Street address only, e.g. 123 Main St.
data_description.state: Two-letter US state/territory code, e.g. VA.
data_description.location_id: Label for this property if you run multiple, e.g. 'Site A'. Sent to Noonlight with every alarm.

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach Noonlight. Check your network and base URL.
error.base_url_required: A custom base URL is required for the 'custom' environment.
error.invalid_phone: Enter a valid phone number, e.g. +12025550142 or (202) 555-0142.
error.invalid_state: Enter a valid 2-letter US state code, e.g. VA.
error.invalid_zip: Enter a valid 5-digit US ZIP code, e.g. 62704.
error.safety_ack_required: You must acknowledge the safety disclosure to use the production environment.

init.title: Noonlight options
init.description: Caller, address, and site changes are made with the Reconfigure action.

data_description.heartbeat_minutes: How often to silently check that Noonlight is reachable and your token is valid, so failures are caught before an emergency. Lower = faster detection, more API calls.

entry_delay_seconds.name: Entry delay (seconds)
entry_delay_seconds.description: Grace window before the dispatch actually fires. Set 0 to skip.

instructions.name: Instructions
instructions.description: Optional context sent to Noonlight responders, e.g. which sensor triggered the alarm. Tip: use a template like 'Triggered by {{ trigger.to_state.name }}'.

account.name: Account
account.description: Config entry id or title. Optional when only one account is configured.

dispatch_all.name: Dispatch all
dispatch_all.description: Request police, fire, and medical in a single alarm.

entry_delay_seconds.name: Entry delay (seconds)
entry_delay_seconds.description: Grace window before the dispatch actually fires. Set 0 to skip.

instructions.name: Instructions
instructions.description: Optional context sent to Noonlight responders, e.g. which sensor triggered the alarm. Tip: use a template like 'Triggered by {{ trigger.to_state.name }}'.

account.name: Account
account.description: Config entry id or title. Optional when only one account is configured.

cancel.name: Cancel
cancel.description: Abort a pending dispatch, or signal Noonlight that an active dispatch is a false alarm.

reason.name: Reason
reason.description: Free-text reason recorded in the audit log.

account.name: Account
account.description: Config entry id or title. Optional when only one account is configured.

test_dispatch.name: Test dispatch
test_dispatch.description: Fire a no-op round-trip against Noonlight's sandbox. Confirms credentials and connectivity without alerting real responders.

account.name: Account
account.description: Config entry id or title. Optional when only one account is configured.

auth_failed.title: Noonlight authentication failed
auth_failed.description: Noonlight rejected the API token. Re-authenticate to restore dispatch capability.

network_failed.title: Cannot reach Noonlight
network_failed.description: Home Assistant could not reach the Noonlight API. Dispatches and status updates may be delayed.

unexpected_response.title: Unexpected Noonlight response
unexpected_response.description: Noonlight returned a response Home Assistant did not understand. Check the logs and the Noonlight status page.
